rationale: The cited paper is used as the source for a perceived-risk measurement scale in the citing paper's empirical design (Study 1b and the appendix), not just as a background mention. The authors explicitly adapt items from Gurhan-Canli and Batra (2004) to test and rule out an alternative explanation based on product risk/safety perceptions. This gives the citation a clear methodological function in how the study is executed and interpreted, even though it is not central to the paper's main theory.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Gurhan-Canli and Batra (2004) by borrowing its perceived-risk scale to operationalize risk concerns in the rescue-based food context. This imported measure is used as a control/robustness element rather than to extend the cited paper's core corporate-image theory.
